headline: No contract-level whitelist or KYC; deposit, withdraw-request, and claim are all permissionless; official ToS is passive boilerplate only

findings.text: The Lido.sol `submit()` function (the ETH deposit entry point) and its payable fallback carry no whitelist, allowlist, `onlyWhitelisted`, `isAccredited`, or `isKYCed` modifiers. The only gate is a DAO-settable staking rate limit (`setStakingLimit`), which caps aggregate inflow per block but is not address-specific. The WithdrawalQueueERC721 `requestWithdrawals()`, `requestWithdrawalsWstETH()`, `requestWithdrawalsWithPermit()`, and `claimWithdrawals()` functions likewise carry no user-facing allowlist. The stETH ERC-20 transfer functions follow standard OpenZeppelin logic with no transfer restrictions.

Deposit (`submit`): permissionless; no off-chain operator approval required to admit the transaction. Withdrawal request (`requestWithdrawals`): permissionless placement; any stETH holder with the appropriate token approval can queue a request with no operator sign-off. Claim of finalized request (`claimWithdrawals`/`claimWithdrawal`): permissionless for the NFT owner once the request is finalized. Finalization itself requires the AccountingOracle committee (currently ~5 operators reaching a 5-of-9 quorum) to submit oracle reports — this is a liveness dependency on downstream settlement, not an admission gate on request placement or claim execution, and is deferred to the dependencies slice. Transfer of stETH: standard ERC-20, unconditional.

A3-passive: The lido.fi ToS (last updated Dec 19, 2025) includes standard eligibility clauses. Preamble states: 'Use of a virtual private network (e.g., a VPN) or other means by ineligible persons to access or use the Interface is prohibited.' Section 7 requires users to represent they are of legal age, comply with applicable laws, and are not subject to relevant sanctions. These are passive self-attestation requirements with no runtime enforcement mechanism visible from static fetch. A3-active: No evidence found of IP geo-blocking, wallet-address screening (Chainalysis/TRM integration), KYC wall, or rendering-blocking jurisdiction banner on stake.lido.fi. The live site is a React SPA which cannot be fully rendered via static fetch, so active enforcement cannot be fully excluded — flagged in unknowns.

A3b-i: Lido documents and maintains IPFS-pinned releases of the Ethereum Staking Widget (https://docs.lido.fi/ipfs/about); these releases are distributions of the official UI and would carry the same ToS. A3b-ii: The Lido homepage explicitly links to third-party integrations including MetaMask Portfolio, Safe App, OKX Web3, Ledger Live, and Cowswap as independent access routes. The Lido SDK (https://github.com/lidofinance/lido-ethereum-sdk) and direct-contract documentation provide programmatic access. The ToS itself (Section 6) states: 'Using the relevant blockchain systems...or the Middleware does not require use of this Interface. Anyone with an internet connection can connect directly to the Middleware or blockchain without accessing or using the Interface.' Multiple documented A3b-ii paths exist.

No onchain OFAC blocklist or third-party sanctions oracle (Chainalysis, TRM, Elliptic) integration was found in the deployed Lido core contracts (stETH proxy 0xae7ab96520DE3A18E5e111B5EaAb095312D7fE84, WithdrawalQueue 0x889edC2eDab5f40e902b864aD4d7AdE8E412F9B1). The ToS contains a sanctions self-attestation eligibility clause but no evidence of onchain enforcement was found.

Read access: fully open — all state-read functions (balance, share price, withdrawal status, oracle data) are callable by any address with no role requirement. Write access: the core user-facing writes (`submit`, `requestWithdrawals`, `requestWithdrawalsWstETH`, `requestWithdrawalsWithPermit`, `claimWithdrawals`, `claimWithdrawal`, ERC-20 transfers) require no privileged role for ordinary users. Privileged write actions (pause via GateSeal 3/6 multisig, finalization via oracle committee, upgrades via DAO vote) apply to protocol-wide state, not to individual user admission.

ToS located at https://lido.fi/terms-of-use, last updated December 19, 2025. Verbatim eligibility preamble: 'Use of a virtual private network (e.g., a VPN) or other means by ineligible persons to access or use the Interface is prohibited, and prohibited uses may attract legal liability for fraudulent use of the Interface.' Section 6 verbatim: 'Using the relevant blockchain systems, third-party supplied wallets, devices, validator nodes or the Middleware does not require use of this Interface. Anyone with an internet connection can connect directly to the Middleware or blockchain without accessing or using the Interface.' Section 7 includes sanctions self-attestation and legal-age eligibility conditions consistent with A3-passive boilerplate.

steelman.red: The DAO-controlled staking rate limit (`setStakingLimit`) can be set to zero by a successful LDO governance vote, effectively blocking all new ETH deposits protocol-wide without any contract-level whitelist — governance-controlled total admission throttle is structurally equivalent to an address-agnostic lockout.
steelman.orange: The official ToS prohibits VPN circumvention by ineligible users and contains a sanctions self-attestation clause; the IPFS-pinned redistributions of the staking widget carry the same Terms, leaving only indirect A3b-ii paths (MetaMask, Safe, OKX, direct-contract) as alternatives to a potentially-enforced official frontend for users in restricted jurisdictions.
steelman.green: No contract-level whitelist, KYC check, or onchain blocklist exists on any user-facing entry or exit function; any Ethereum address can deposit ETH, request withdrawals, and claim finalized withdrawals directly without operator approval; multiple well-documented independent A3b-ii access paths (MetaMask, Safe, SDK, direct contract) are publicly available; and the ToS itself explicitly states that use of the Interface is not required to interact with the protocol.

rationale.verdict: Choosing green because: (1) zero contract-level admission restrictions on deposit, withdrawal-request, or claim functions for any Ethereum address; (2) the ToS contains only passive attestation requirements with no evidence of runtime enforcement (A3-active could not be proven); (3) the official ToS itself documents that direct-contract access bypasses the Interface entirely; and (4) multiple independent A3b-ii paths are prominently documented and active. The staking rate limit and oracle-finalization dependency are real constraints but are protocol-wide liveness/governance matters (belonging in control and dependencies slices), not address-specific admission gates. The green steel-man is materially stronger than orange or red.

evidence.shows: Deployed Lido stETH proxy contract on Ethereum mainnet; shows AppProxyUpgradeable proxy at 0xae7ab96520DE3A18E5e111B5EaAb095312D7fE84 pointing to implementation 0x6ca84080381E43938476814be61B779A8bB6a600; verifies the contract is live and accessible

Canonical deployed-contracts index listing all core protocol addresses including Lido/stETH (0xae7ab96520DE3A18E5e111B5EaAb095312D7fE84), WithdrawalQueueERC721 (0x889edC2eDab5f40e902b864aD4d7AdE8E412F9B1), and Lido Locator (0xC1d0b3DE6792Bf6b4b37EccdcC24e45978Cfd2Eb); confirms protocol version v3.0.1 active on Ethereum mainnet

Source of WithdrawalQueue.sol showing requestWithdrawals(), requestWithdrawalsWstETH(), requestWithdrawalsWithPermit() have no user whitelist modifier; only pauseUntil() carries onlyRole(PAUSE_ROLE); confirms user-facing deposit and withdrawal functions are permissionless at the contract level

Source of Lido.sol showing PAUSE_ROLE and RESUME_ROLE are defined but user-facing submit()/fallback have no allowlist modifier; confirms staking rate limit is aggregate (not address-gated) and deposit is permissionless at the contract level

Full ToS text (last updated Dec 19, 2025); Section 6 explicitly states direct-contract access does not require the Interface; Section 7 contains passive self-attestation eligibility requirements (legal age, sanctions compliance, technical knowledge); preamble contains VPN prohibition clause

Documents that superuser actions on stETH (pause, upgrade, fee changes) require successful LDO DAO votes (5% quorum, 50% support, 120-hour voting); confirms no single operator can gate user entry/exit unilaterally; token management roles belong to smart contracts, not EOAs

Documents official IPFS deployments of the Lido Ethereum Staking Widget with CID-verified releases; confirms A3b-i IPFS redistribution path; notes future plan to anchor latest CID on-chain via DAO governance

Documents GateSeal Committee (3/6 multisig at 0x8772E3a2D86B9347A2688f9bc1808A6d8917760C) authorized to trigger GateSeal contracts for time-limited pause of WithdrawalQueue and ValidatorExitBus; pause is single-use and expiry-capped; confirms pause power is held by a 3/6 identified-signer multisig, not a single EOA
Deployed WithdrawalQueueERC721 proxy contract on Ethereum mainnet at canonical address from Lido docs; verifies the contract is live
Lido Bug Bounty program on Immunefi with up to $2M rewards; confirms public bug bounty URL
Lido security documentation pointing to Immunefi bug bounty program; confirms bug bounty URL

unknowns: A3-active: Could not fully verify whether stake.lido.fi applies active runtime enforcement (IP geo-blocking, wallet-address screening via Chainalysis/TRM, or rendering-blocking jurisdiction banner) because the site is a React SPA that could not be fully rendered via static HTTP fetch; no verbatim block-banner or named third-party screening provider was found, so A3-active is not asserted, but it cannot be categorically excluded from the live application layer.

A4: Cannot exclude that the official frontend applies a server-side sanctions screening API call (not visible in on-chain contract code) that rejects transactions from flagged wallets at the UI layer before broadcasting; this would be frontend-only enforcement, not on-chain, but was not verifiable from the static fetch.

admin_addresses.role: GateSeal Committee – can trigger time-limited pause of WithdrawalQueue and ValidatorExitBus via GateSeal contracts (3/6 multisig, single-use pause right)
Emergency Brakes – can disable wstETH bridge deposits/withdrawals and pause Easy Track pipeline (3/5 multisig)
Lido stETH proxy – upgradeable; implementation changeable via DAO vote
WithdrawalQueueERC721 proxy – PAUSE_ROLE held by GateSeal contract; FINALIZE_ROLE held by Lido accounting
